document_title: Diploma in Teacher Education - Science and Technology Curriculum

sub_strands.name: 1.1 The Nature of Science
specific_learning_outcomes: Explain the meaning of science as a body of knowledge and a process
Describe the scientific method
Appreciate the role of science and technology in society
suggested_learning_experiences: Discuss different definitions of science.
Design and conduct a simple experiment using the scientific method (e.g., testing which paper airplane design flies farthest).
Brainstorm technological innovations that have changed daily life.

sub_strands.name: 2.1 Classification of Living Things
specific_learning_outcomes: Classify living things into major kingdoms (plants, animals, fungi, etc.)
Differentiate between vertebrates and invertebrates
Differentiate between flowering and non-flowering plants
suggested_learning_experiences: Create posters or digital presentations classifying local plants and animals.
Go on a nature walk to identify different types of plants and animals.
Use dichotomous keys to identify organisms.
core_competencies: Learning to learn and reflective practice

sub_strands.name: 3.1 Properties of Matter
specific_learning_outcomes: Define matter
Describe the three states of matter: solid, liquid, gas
Explain changes of state (melting, freezing, evaporation, condensation)
suggested_learning_experiences: Observe the melting of ice and the boiling of water.
Draw particle models for solids, liquids, and gases.
Conduct an experiment to show that air has mass.

sub_strands.name: 3.2 Forms of Energy
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify different forms of energy (heat, light, sound, electrical, kinetic, potential)
Describe energy transformations from one form to another
suggested_learning_experiences: Build a simple circuit to demonstrate electrical energy transforming into light and heat.
Identify energy transformations in everyday objects (e.g., a flashlight, a bouncing ball).
Create an 'energy chain' diagram.

sub_strands.name: 4.1 Concept of Force
specific_learning_outcomes: Define force as a push or a pull
Identify effects of force on an object (start/stop motion, change direction, change shape)
Differentiate between contact forces (e.g., friction) and non-contact forces (e.g., gravity, magnetism)
suggested_learning_experiences: Push and pull objects in the classroom to experience force.
Drop objects to observe the effect of gravity.
Use magnets to demonstrate non-contact force.
Investigate how friction affects the movement of a toy car on different surfaces.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to define force and identify its effects

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Comprehensively defines force with varied examples and clearly explains all its effects.
levels.Meets Expectation: Correctly defines force as a push or a pull and identifies its main effects on an object.
levels.Approaches Expectation: Can define force but only identifies some of its effects.
levels.Below Expectation: Requires assistance to define force and its effects.

sub_strands.name: 4.2 Simple Machines
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify the six types of simple machines (lever, wheel and axle, pulley, inclined plane, wedge, screw)
Explain how simple machines make work easier
Construct a simple machine using locally available materials
suggested_learning_experiences: Identify simple machines in the school environment.
Build a lever using a ruler and a fulcrum to lift an object.
Design and create a pulley system to lift a small weight.
Discuss the concept of mechanical advantage.

sub_strands.name: 4.3 Magnetism
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify materials that are attracted by a magnet (magnetic materials)
Describe the properties of a magnet (poles, attraction/repulsion)
Make a temporary magnet
suggested_learning_experiences: Test various classroom objects to see if they are magnetic.
Experiment with bar magnets to identify North and South poles and observe attraction and repulsion.
Make a temporary magnet by stroking a nail with a permanent magnet.
Plot the magnetic field of a bar magnet using iron filings or a compass.
core_competencies: Learning to learn and reflective practice

sub_strands.name: 5.1 The Solar System
specific_learning_outcomes: Name the components of the solar system (Sun, planets, moons, asteroids)
Describe the relative positions and movements of the Earth, Sun, and Moon
Explain the causes of day and night, and seasons
suggested_learning_experiences: Build a physical or digital model of the solar system.
Use a globe and a light source to demonstrate how the Earth's rotation causes day and night.
Dramatize the orbit of the Earth around the Sun and the Moon around the Earth.

sub_strands.name: 6.1 Inquiry-Based Science Education
specific_learning_outcomes: Explain the principles of inquiry-based learning in a science context
Facilitate a science lesson using the 5E Instructional Model (Engage, Explore, Explain, Elaborate, Evaluate)
Develop inquiry questions to guide scientific investigation
suggested_learning_experiences: Analyse a lesson plan that uses the 5E model.
Deliver a micro-lesson for a science topic using an inquiry-based approach.
Peer-review inquiry questions for clarity and investigability.
